verses.latin_vulgate: Onus Damasci.   Ecce Damascus desinet esse civitas,  et erit sicut acervus lapidum in ruina.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַשָּׂ֖א דַּמָּ֑שֶׂק הִנֵּ֤ה דַמֶּ֙שֶׂק֙ מוּסָ֣ר מֵעִ֔יר וְהָיְתָ֖ה מְעִ֥י מַפָּלָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The burden of Damascus. Behold, Damascus is taken away from being a city, and it shall be a ruinous heap.

verses.latin_vulgate: Derelictæ civitates Aroër  gregibus erunt,  et requiescent ibi, et non erit qui exterreat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲזֻב֖וֹת עָרֵ֣י עֲרֹעֵ֑ר לַעֲדָרִ֣ים תִּֽהְיֶ֔ינָה וְרָבְצ֖וּ וְאֵ֥ין מַחֲרִֽיד׃
verses.king_james: The cities of Aroer are forsaken: they shall be for flocks, which shall lie down, and none shall make them afraid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cessabit adjutorium ab Ephraim,  et regnum a Damasco ;  et reliquiæ Syriæ  sicut gloria filiorum Israël erunt,  dicit Dominus exercituum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנִשְׁבַּ֤ת מִבְצָר֙ מֵאֶפְרַ֔יִם וּמַמְלָכָ֥ה מִדַּמֶּ֖שֶׂק וּשְׁאָ֣ר אֲרָ֑ם כִּכְב֤וֹד בְּנֵֽי־יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ יִֽהְי֔וּ נְאֻ֖ם יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָאֽוֹת׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: The fortress also shall cease from Ephraim, and the kingdom from Damascus, and the remnant of Syria: they shall be as the glory of the children of Israel, saith the Lord of hosts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erit in die illa :  attenuabitur gloria Jacob,  et pinguedo carnis ejus marcescet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָה֙ בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֔וּא יִדַּ֖ל כְּב֣וֹד יַעֲקֹ֑ב וּמִשְׁמַ֥ן בְּשָׂר֖וֹ יֵרָזֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: And in that day it shall come to pass, that the glory of Jacob shall be made thin, and the fatness of his flesh shall wax lean.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erit sicut congregans in messe quod restiterit,  et brachium ejus spicas leget ;  et erit sicut quærens spicas  in valle Raphaim.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָ֗ה כֶּֽאֱסֹף֙ קָצִ֣יר קָמָ֔ה וּזְרֹע֖וֹ שִׁבֳּלִ֣ים יִקְצ֑וֹר וְהָיָ֛ה כִּמְלַקֵּ֥ט שִׁבֳּלִ֖ים בְּעֵ֥מֶק רְפָאִֽים׃
verses.king_james: And it shall be as when the harvestman gathereth the corn, and reapeth the ears with his arm; and it shall be as he that gathereth ears in the valley of Rephaim.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et relinquetur in eo sicut racemus et sicut excussio oleæ  duarum vel trium olivarum in summitate rami,  sive quatuor aut quinque in cacuminibus ejus fructus ejus,  dicit Dominus Deus Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנִשְׁאַר־בּ֤וֹ עֽוֹלֵלֹת֙ כְּנֹ֣קֶף זַ֔יִת שְׁנַ֧יִם שְׁלֹשָׁ֛ה גַּרְגְּרִ֖ים בְּרֹ֣אשׁ אָמִ֑יר אַרְבָּעָ֣ה חֲמִשָּׁ֗ה בִּסְעִפֶ֙יהָ֙ פֹּֽרִיָּ֔ה נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֖ה אֱלֹהֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: Yet gleaning grapes shall be left in it, as the shaking of an olive tree, two or three berries in the top of the uppermost bough, four or five in the outmost fruitful branches thereof, saith the Lord God of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: In die illa inclinabitur homo ad  factorem suum,  et oculi ejus ad Sanctum Israël respicient ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֔וּא יִשְׁעֶ֥ה הָאָדָ֖ם עַל־עֹשֵׂ֑הוּ וְעֵינָ֕יו אֶל־קְד֥וֹשׁ יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל תִּרְאֶֽינָה׃
verses.king_james: At that day shall a man look to his Maker, and his eyes shall have respect to the Holy One of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: et non inclinabitur ad altaria quæ fecerunt manus ejus ;  et quæ operati sunt digiti ejus non respiciet  lucos et delubra.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹ֣א יִשְׁעֶ֔ה אֶל־הַֽמִּזְבְּח֖וֹת מַעֲשֵׂ֣ה יָדָ֑יו וַאֲשֶׁ֨ר עָשׂ֤וּ אֶצְבְּעֹתָיו֙ לֹ֣א יִרְאֶ֔ה וְהָאֲשֵׁרִ֖ים וְהָחַמָּנִֽים׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: And he shall not look to the altars, the work of his hands, neither shall respect that which his fingers have made, either the groves, or the images.

verses.latin_vulgate: In die illa erunt civitates fortitudinis ejus  derelictæ sicut aratra, et segetes  quæ derelictæ sunt a facie filiorum Israël ;  et eris deserta.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּיּ֨וֹם הַה֜וּא יִהְי֣וּ ׀ עָרֵ֣י מָעוּזּ֗וֹ כַּעֲזוּבַ֤ת הַחֹ֙רֶשׁ֙ וְהָ֣אָמִ֔יר אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָֽזְב֔וּ מִפְּנֵ֖י בְּנֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וְהָיְתָ֖ה שְׁמָמָֽה׃
verses.king_james: In that day shall his strong cities be as a forsaken bough, and an uppermost branch, which they left because of the children of Israel: and there shall be desolation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia oblitus es Dei salvatoris tui,  et fortis adjutoris tui non es recordata :  propterea plantabis plantationem fidelem,  et germen alienum seminabis ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י שָׁכַ֙חַתְּ֙ אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׁעֵ֔ךְ וְצ֥וּר מָעֻזֵּ֖ךְ לֹ֣א זָכָ֑רְתְּ עַל־כֵּ֗ן תִּטְּעִי֙ נִטְעֵ֣י נַעֲמָנִ֔ים וּזְמֹ֥רַת זָ֖ר תִּזְרָעֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: Because thou hast forgotten the God of thy salvation, and hast not been mindful of the rock of thy strength, therefore shalt thou plant pleasant plants, and shalt set it with strange slips:

verses.latin_vulgate: in die plantationis tuæ labrusca,  et mane semen tuum florebit ;  ablata est messis in die hæreditatis,  et dolebit graviter.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּי֤וֹם נִטְעֵךְ֙ תְּשַׂגְשֵׂ֔גִי וּבַבֹּ֖קֶר זַרְעֵ֣ךְ תַּפְרִ֑יחִי נֵ֥ד קָצִ֛יר בְּי֥וֹם נַחֲלָ֖ה וּכְאֵ֥ב אָנֽוּשׁ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: In the day shalt thou make thy plant to grow, and in the morning shalt thou make thy seed to flourish: but the harvest shall be a heap in the day of grief and of desperate sorrow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ multitudini populorum multorum,  ut multitudo maris sonantis ;  et tumultus turbarum,  sicut sonitus aquarum multarum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֗וֹי הֲמוֹן֙ עַמִּ֣ים רַבִּ֔ים כַּהֲמ֥וֹת יַמִּ֖ים יֶהֱמָי֑וּן וּשְׁא֣וֹן לְאֻמִּ֔ים כִּשְׁא֛וֹן מַ֥יִם כַּבִּירִ֖ים יִשָּׁאֽוּן׃
verses.king_james: Woe to the multitude of many people, which make a noise like the noise of the seas; and to the rushing of nations, that make a rushing like the rushing of mighty waters!

verses.latin_vulgate: Sonabunt populi sicut sonitus aquarum inundantium,  et increpabit eum, et fugiet procul ;  et rapietur sicut pulvis montium a facie venti,  et sicut turbo coram tempestate.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְאֻמִּ֗ים כִּשְׁא֞וֹן מַ֤יִם רַבִּים֙ יִשָּׁא֔וּן וְגָ֥עַר בּ֖וֹ וְנָ֣ס מִמֶּרְחָ֑ק וְרֻדַּ֗ף כְּמֹ֤ץ הָרִים֙ לִפְנֵי־ר֔וּחַ וּכְגַלְגַּ֖ל לִפְנֵ֥י סוּפָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The nations shall rush like the rushing of many waters: but God shall rebuke them, and they shall flee far off, and shall be chased as the chaff of the mountains before the wind, and like a rolling thing before the whirlwind.

verses.latin_vulgate: In tempore vespere, et ecce turbatio ;  in matutino, et non subsistet.  Hæc est pars eorum qui vastaverunt nos,  et sors diripientium nos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְעֵ֥ת עֶ֙רֶב֙ וְהִנֵּ֣ה בַלָּהָ֔ה בְּטֶ֥רֶם בֹּ֖קֶר אֵינֶ֑נּוּ זֶ֚ה חֵ֣לֶק שׁוֹסֵ֔ינוּ וְגוֹרָ֖ל לְבֹזְזֵֽינוּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And behold at eveningtide trouble; and before the morning he is not. This is the portion of them that spoil us, and the lot of them that rob us.
